response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

***  

the approach to solve this problem is to iterate through each element in the array and calculate the required sum based on the given conditions. for each index i, if i is a multiple of 3, we calculate the sum of squares of differences between nums[i] and all other elements. otherwise, we calculate the sum of absolute differences.  

to efficiently compute the sum of absolute differences, we can use the fact that the array is sorted. for a sorted array, the sum of absolute differences can be computed using prefix sums. for the square of differences, we can use a similar approach but with squared values.  

the code will iterate through each element, check if the index is a multiple of 3, and compute the appropriate sum. the final result array will be returned with the computed values.  

the letter k should appear at least 2 times.
